data.title: Texas jury orders Musk's X to pay $105M to VidStream in tech patent case
data.description:
A Texas jury on Wednesday found Elon Musk's social media platform X guilty of infringing VidStream LLC's technology and has ordered the company to pay $105M.

VidStream said that X, along with now defunct Vine and Periscope, violated its technologies for creating video content and had initially sought to get over $600M in damages.

VidStream filed a case against the company, when it was still known as Twitter, for IP infringement in 2016. They alleged that the social media platform's video services violated technologies that allowed for "rapidly creating and distributing user-generated video content over a variety of networks."

According to the lawsuit, the technology patents let users make video content for distribution on social media platforms, webpages, and television broadcasts.

X tried to defend itself, saying its technology was different from VidStream's and that the patents were invalid.

The case is VidStream LLC v. Twitter Inc., Civil Action No. 3:16-cv-00764, filed in the U.S. District Court, Northern District of Texas, Dallas Division.

More on X

  • Musk's X could be fined $1B+ for breaching EU's DSA law - NYT
  • Elon Musk says xAI has acquired X in all-stock deal
  • X adds more companies to lawsuit over advertising boycott: report
  • Banks prepare to sell billions of dollars of X loans: report
  • Elon Musk's X partners with Visa in financial services push

data.title: Charles Schwab stock gains as Q1 earnings beat, core net new assets climb
data.description:
Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) stock gained 3.7% in Thursday premarket trading after the firm's Q1 core net new assets climbed 44% Y/Y amid volatile markets.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.04, topping the consensus estimate of $1.01, rose from $1.01 in Q4 2024 and $0.74 in last year’s Q1.

Net revenue of $5.60B, beating the $5.54B consensus, increased from $5.33B in the prior quarter and $4.74B a year ago.

Net interest revenue of $2.71B, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.64B, grew from $2.53B in Q4 and $2.23B in Q1 2024.

Asset management and administration fees were $1.53B vs. $1.51B in the previous quarter and $1.35B a year ago.

Average bank deposit account balances of $84.3B in March increased from $83.1B in February and declined from $85.4B in December.

For Q1, core net new assets were $137.7B, an annualized growth rate of 5.5%. "This 44% year-over-year increase in asset gathering was powered by our unwavering focus on serving the needs of clients across Retail, Advisor Services, and Workplace Financial Services," said President and CEO Rick Wurster. Core net new assets of $59.1B in March 2025 rose 23% M/M and 31% Y/Y.

Client transactional sweep cash balances ended the quarter at $407.8B, a Q/Q drop of $10.8B, reflecting typical Q1 seasonality.

The firm's bank supplemental funding declined $11.8B during the quarter to $38.1B at March month-end.

Q1 average money market fund client assets of $621.5B vs. $581.0B in the prior quarter and $499.9B a year ago.

Total expenses, excluding interest, were $3.14B, vs. $3.02B in the previous quarter and $2.94B a year ago.

Earlier, Charles Schwab Non-GAAP EPS of $1.04 beats by $0.03, revenue of $5.6B beats by $60M

data.title: Enovix appoints Ryan Benton as chief financial officer
data.description:
• Enovix (NASDAQ:ENVX) has appointed Ryan Benton as chief financial officer, the company said on Thursday.
  • Benton brings over three decades of financial leadership experience. He previously held key roles at ASM International and served as CFO for multiple public companies including Silvaco and Exar Corporation.
  • Source: Press Release

data.description:
• Elisa Oyj press release (OTCPK:ELMUF): Q1 GAAP EPS of €0.58.

data.title: Utz appoints William J. Kelley Jr. as CFO
data.description:
•  Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) announced on Thursday the appointment of William J. Kelley Jr. as Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer and Principal Accounting Officer, effective May 1, 2025. 
  • He will succeed Ajay Kataria, who will step down on May 1, 2025.
  • Utz also announced that Mark Schreiber, Executive Vice President, Sales & Chief Customer Officer, will retire effective May 31, 2025, and will remain in a transition role through the end of June. He will be succeeded by Jeremy Stuart, currently Senior Vice President, Large Format.
  • Utz also reaffirmed the 2025 fiscal year outlook and announced its preliminary first quarter 2025 results.
  • For the first quarter of 2025 the company expects:
  1. -Net Sales to increase between 1.5% and 1.7% versus the prior year period
  2. -Organic Net Sales to increase between 2.8% and 3.0% versus the prior year period
  3. -Net Income of between $4 million and $6 million
  4. -Adjusted EBITDA of between $44 million and $46 million
  5. -Diluted Earnings Per Share of between $0.08 and $0.10
  6. -Adjusted Earnings Per Share of between $0.14 and $0.16

data.title: Silver Elephant Mining upsizes private placement to $540,000
data.description:
• Silver Elephant Mining (TSX:ELEF:CA) announced on Thursday that it proposes to amend the terms of the non-brokered private placement originally announced on April 1, 2025.
  • The company said the private placement will be increased to raise gross proceeds of up to CAD540,000 through the sale of 3 million units at $0.18 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share of the company and one share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional share at $0.30 per share for a period of three years from issuance.
  • John Lee, a director of the company, will be subscribing for 500,000 Units for gross proceeds of $90,000.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Badger Meter GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.27, revenue of $222.2M beats by $1.44M
data.description:
• Badger Meter press release (NYSE:BMI): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $222.2M (+13.2% Y/Y) beats by $1.44M.
  • The tax rate for the first quarter of 2025 was 24.4%, modestly above the prior year’s 23.5%. 

  • Outlook: Kenneth C. Bockhorst, Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer said,"Our first quarter results demonstrate the resilience and durability of our replacement-driven business amidst a volatile macroeconomic environment. As previously communicated, we will face difficult prior-year comparisons in the upcoming second quarter of 2025. Nevertheless, the attractive fundamentals of the water industry, and our ability to add value to customers with an innovative and reliable portfolio of solutions, support our average high-single-digit long-term revenue growth outlook."

data.title: Philly Fed drops to -26.4, lowest reading since April 2023
data.description:
The Philly Fed Manufacturing Index dropped to -26.4 in April, vs. 6.7 consensus, from 12.5 in the prior month.

The diffusion index for current general activity dropped 39 points to -26.4 in April, its lowest reading since April 2023.

The index for new orders also fell sharply, from 8.7 in March to -34.2 this month, its lowest reading since April 2020.

The current shipments index decreased 11 points to -9.1 this month.

Employment: 0.20 vs. 19.7.

Prices Paid: 63.1 vs. 48.3.
